macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Fed cuts rates 25 bps and ends balance-sheet runoff amid shutdown-limited data
items.summary: The Federal Reserve lowered interest rates by another 0.25 percentage point and announced it will stop shrinking its balance sheet. The decision drew two dissents in opposite directions, underscoring internal debate over whether policy should be eased further or held steady.

items.title: US government shutdown deal reached to reopen operations through January
items.summary: Congressional parties reportedly reached a deal to reopen the government at least through the end of January, which would restore federal workers to their jobs with back pay and reverse some shutdown-era layoffs. The agreement also leaves room for a Senate vote on extending enhanced Obamacare subsidies.

items.title: September CPI came in slightly below expectations, but inflation remains above target
items.summary: The latest CPI data showed headline inflation rising 0.3% month over month versus 0.4% expected, while core CPI rose 0.2% versus 0.3% expected. On a year-over-year basis, both headline and core inflation were reported at 3.0%, still above the Fed’s 2% target.

items.title: U.S. labor market data mixed as ADP and Challenger reports point to slowing conditions
items.summary: Recent U.S. labor-market indicators were mixed, with ADP employment change at 109K against a 61K consensus and Challenger job cuts at 60.62K. Traders are watching these releases closely because the shutdown has limited the flow of official data.

items.title: Tariff debate continues as economists assess limited near-term damage to growth
items.summary: A Brookings analysis argues that the second Trump administration’s tariff increases are historically large, but the broader economy has so far not shown severe damage. The piece suggests that pass-through effects, demand shifts, and policy offsets may be muting the immediate impact.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin nears $82,000 as U.S. crypto bill clears Senate committee
items.summary: Bitcoin traded near $81,400 and briefly touched $82,000 after a Senate committee advanced the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act on a 15-9 vote. The move came alongside more than $1 billion in spot trading volume and renewed bullish momentum across crypto markets.

items.title: Bitcoin jumps above $82,000 amid short squeeze and regulatory optimism
items.summary: Bitcoin surged more than $2,000 in four hours and broke above $82,000, with reporting attributing part of the move to a $145 million short squeeze. The rally also followed progress on the Clarity Act and rising institutional interest.

items.title: JPMorgan says Bitcoin remains the clear institutional winner in crypto
items.summary: JPMorgan analysts said Bitcoin continues to outperform Ether and altcoins across key institutional metrics, even as the broader market rebounds. The bank expects crypto inflows to exceed 2025 levels in 2026, led increasingly by institutions.

items.title: CME Group plans first multi-crypto index futures product
items.summary: CME Group said it plans to launch Nasdaq CME Crypto Index futures on June 8, pending regulatory approval. The product would give institutional investors exposure to a basket of major cryptocurrencies including Bitcoin, Ether, XRP and Solana.

items.title: Strategy’s STRC preferred stock tops $1.24 billion in issuance volume
items.summary: Strategy Inc.’s STRC program continued scaling its Bitcoin accumulation effort, with live trackers showing more than $1.24 billion in issuance volume and an estimated 11,709 BTC acquired. The structure has become one of the largest corporate Bitcoin-buying vehicles on record.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Capcom reports record annual sales, driven by Resident Evil
items.summary: Capcom said it sold more games in its latest fiscal year than in any year in its history, with Resident Evil helping drive the strong results. The company highlighted the series’ continued strength across its back catalog and newer releases.

items.title: Red Dead Redemption 2 added to May’s PlayStation Plus game catalog
items.summary: Sony is adding Red Dead Redemption 2 to the PlayStation Plus game catalog this month, alongside Star Wars Outlaws and other titles. The lineup gives subscribers a mix of blockbuster and recent releases to claim and play.

items.title: Nintendo headquarters threatened in Japan, prompting arrest
items.summary: A Japanese man was arrested after allegedly threatening to blow up Nintendo’s headquarters. The incident follows similar threats that previously forced Nintendo to cancel live events in 2024.

items.title: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced sets up a real-world treasure hunt
items.summary: Ubisoft’s Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced promotion will include a real-life treasure hunt with a prize valued at half a million dollars. The hunt is scheduled to begin later this year and could run for two to five years.

items.title: Saros sales seen as soft after two-week launch, analyst estimates
items.summary: An analytics firm estimates that Saros sold just over 300,000 copies in its first two weeks, describing the launch as lukewarm. The report suggests the game may struggle to break even, though players who bought it are spending significant time with it.

items.title: Indiana Jones and the Great Circle shown running on Switch 2
items.summary: Video footage shared by VGC shows Indiana Jones and the Great Circle running on Switch 2 in both docked and handheld modes. The footage suggests one of 2024’s standout games is already being demonstrated on Nintendo’s next console.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 studio warns about bans after pirated leak
items.summary: Playground Games said it will hand out franchise-wide and hardware bans to anyone caught playing a pirated leaked build of Forza Horizon 6. The studio also said the leak was not caused by a preloading issue.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Sony's Xperia 1 VIII Features Bigger Camera Sensors and New Design
items.summary: Sony has unveiled its latest flagship smartphone with enhanced camera sensors and a redesigned form factor, though the company continues to exclude the US market from its release plans.

items.title: Microsoft's Unreleased Cloud Controller Spotted in the Wild
items.summary: A compact cloud controller for Xbox Cloud Gaming has been discovered, reportedly using Wi-Fi for direct connection to reduce latency during gameplay.

items.title: Spotify Adopts Apple's HTTP Live Streaming for Video Podcasts
items.summary: Spotify and its hosting platforms are implementing Apple's video podcast technology, enabling Apple Podcasts to play Spotify-hosted video content.

items.title: Microsoft Retires Copilot Mode on Edge Browser
items.summary: Microsoft is discontinuing the dedicated Copilot Mode on Edge as Copilot features are now integrated throughout the browser experience, with mobile support added.

items.title: AMD Launches AI-Targeted PCIe Cards for Current Servers
items.summary: AMD has released the Instinct MI350P PCIe cards designed for companies seeking gradual AI investment without major hardware overhauls.

items.title: AWS Experiences US-East-1 Outage Due to Data Center Cooling Failure
items.summary: A thermal event in AWS's primary US region caused a cooling system failure, leading to server overheating and service impairments to EC2 and EBS.

items.title: PCI Express 8.0 Specification Targets High-Bandwidth Applications
items.summary: The PCI group has begun development on the PCI Express 8.0 specification scheduled for 2028, designed to support bandwidth-intensive applications and edge computing.

items.title: Insta360 Launches Retro-Themed Go 3S Bundle at Lower Price Point
items.summary: Insta360 is releasing a special edition Go 3S Retro Bundle that offers nostalgic styling at a price lower than the previous model.

items.title: US Carriers Partner to Improve Satellite Connectivity
items.summary: The three major US carriers are collaborating to enhance satellite connectivity coverage across the country.
